mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, consumer spending power deteriorates sharply, causing corporate earnings revisions to turn negative and driving S&P 500 forward returns down over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicate robust aggregate demand without inflationary pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of triggered months or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >55% of observations during confirmed expansionary periods, or the predictive coefficient flips negative.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of months following housing starts increases, or the series shows zero predictive power across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, inflationary pressures force monetary authorities to consider tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL breaches the 235.0 threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, increasing consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth indicates strengthening economic fundamentals, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of quarters during recovery regimes, or the predictive relationship reverses when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annualized.
